commentaries.title: Rates Rally Extends but Curve Distortions Deepen
commentaries.body:
Bangladesh government securities posted broad-based gains this week, with yields declining 8–15 basis points across most tenors. The front end continues to lead the rally as the market prices in a dovish pivot from Bangladesh Bank.

**Bill Segment: Strong Demand, Sixth Consecutive Weekly Decline**

- 364D T-bill cleared at 10.07%, down 11bps — marking the sixth consecutive weekly decline and the longest streak since pre-tightening
- 91D T-bill printed at 9.85%, now firmly below the 10% repo rate for the first time this cycle, with bid-cover at 1.92x
- 182D cleared at 10.20% with 1.75x coverage — solid demand across the entire bill curve

**Bond Segment: Duration Hesitancy Persists**

- 5Y BGTB cleared at 10.82% with a respectable 1.6x cover
- 10Y auction revealed continued reluctance — a tepid 1.4x bid-cover forced 10% devolvement (৳200 Cr) onto primary dealers, even as the cutoff fell 10bps to 11.15%
- 20Y BGTB auction saw ৳250 Cr devolvement with only 1.1x bid-cover — institutional buyers remain unwilling to extend duration with CPI at 9.13% compressing real returns

**Curve Shape: Three Major Distortions**

The curve presents severe anomalies that ALCO desks must navigate carefully:

- **182D/364D inversion**: 182D at 10.20% trades through 364D at 10.07% — a 13bp inversion suggesting front-end supply/demand imbalance
- **2Y/5Y belly inversion**: The 2Y at 11.95% sits a massive 113bps above the 5Y at 10.82% — the most pronounced belly inversion in recent memory, pointing to acute term premium dislocation or mispricing
- **15Y/20Y kink**: The 15Y at 12.68% trades 103bps above the 20Y at 11.65%, creating a sharp ultra-long hump that defies smooth interpolation

**Macro Backdrop**

The supportive environment for easing remains intact:

- Repo rate corridor at 10% (SDF 7.50% – SLF 11.50%) anchors the front end
- FX reserves at $35.1 billion — a dramatic recovery from under $20B a year ago — with BB now a net USD buyer ($4.3B purchased since July 2025)
- USD/BDT stable at 121.20, taka broadly flat
- Excess banking sector liquidity at ৳3.35 lakh crore, with over 70% parked in government securities
- Call money rate at 9.80%, below the repo rate

**Watchpoint**: The 2Y/5Y inversion is the market's biggest puzzle. Until devolvement clears at the long end and the belly normalises, duration extension should remain selective. The April inflation print is the key catalyst — any break below 9% could trigger the next leg of the rally and finally draw buyers into the 10Y+ segment.
keyPoints: 91D T-Bill: 9.85% — now below the 10% repo rate
2Y/5Y belly inversion at 113bps — largest in recent memory
FX reserves: $35.1B, BB net buyer of $4.3B since Jul 2025
commentaries.curveShapeNote: Severely distorted — three inversions (182D/364D, 2Y/5Y, 15Y/20Y). Not a normal curve.
commentaries.outlook: Duration extension should remain selective until belly inversion resolves. Watch April CPI — a break below 9% could catalyze the next rally leg.

commentaries.title: Front End Breaks Below Repo as Easing Bets Build
commentaries.body:
The 91D T-bill broke below 10% for the first time since Bangladesh Bank's tightening cycle began, signaling the market is now pricing rate cuts as a near certainty. Meanwhile, excess liquidity continues to build — banks are flush with deposits but reluctant to lend given NPLs at 31.8%. Government borrowing absorbed some liquidity in recent weeks but not enough to prevent the structural surplus from growing.

**Key Moves**

- 91D fell to 9.90%, breaching the psychological 10% level
- 364D at 10.12%, down 16bps from a month ago
- 5Y at 10.87%, the belly continues to compress faster than the long end
- 10Y steady at 11.20% — long-end remains sticky

The market is in a classic two-speed mode: aggressive front-end buying versus cautious long-end positioning. Until inflation convincingly breaks below 9%, expect this pattern to persist.
keyPoints: 91D T-Bill breaks below 10% for first time this cycle
364D at 10.12% — 16bps lower than a month ago
10Y sticky at 11.20% — duration buyers remain cautious
Excess liquidity at ৳3.35L Cr and climbing
commentaries.curveShapeNote: Front-end rally outpacing the long end — curve steepening with belly distortion.
commentaries.outlook: The 91D sub-10% print is the clearest signal yet that markets expect BB to cut. Position for continued front-end compression.
